narratives.full_description:
Trilogy of contemplative music performances created in collaboration with Keron Foundation at Zvartnots International Airport

“Arrival” — the first in a trilogy of contemplative music performances by the hosq Foundation at Zvartnots International Airport.

The hosq  Foundation, in collaboration with Keron Foundation, presents a series of three contemplative musical performances within the airport environment. 
 Here, contemplation means listening in the same way one watches: noticing rhythms, pauses, and subtle shifts of state that usually remain in the background. 
 
 The trilogy’s title refers to Brian Eno’s Music for Airports, the work that helped define and consolidate ambient music as a genre, and affirmed the idea of music as an environment. This musical performance is an instrument of perception: it supports a gentle experience of transition and changes the quality of the space, making it more audible and more gathered. By entering public space through a curated performance, we invites  arrivals, departures, and guests to become part of a shared, unfolding experience of listening. 
 
 Each performance is newly composed for this context and created specifically for this occasion. 
 
 hosq × Yerevan State Conservatory 
 
 As a strategic partner of hosq  Foundation, the Yerevan State Conservatory plays a key role in the realization of the first performance of the trilogy. The featured artists are students and alumni of the Conservatory, invited to engage with contemporary musical practices outside traditional concert settings. 
 
 This collaboration creates a bridge between academic musical education and experimental. By placing young musicians in a public environment such as the airport, the project opens new professional horizons and offers a rare opportunity to perform contemporary music in dialogue with everyday life and movement. 
 
 Through this partnership, hosq  and the Conservatory jointly support the development of emerging artists, encouraging them to explore new formats of listening, authorship, and presence: where performance becomes part of a shared sonic and social experience rather than a closed event.
narratives.internationality_narrative:
Zvartnots International Airport of Yerevan, is the main air gate of the Republic of Armenia, which has been operating since 1961.

narratives.post_effect_continuation_narrative:
https://conservatory.am 
 
 Yerevan State Conservatory (YSC) after Komitas was founded in 1921 as a music studio and re-founded two years later as a higher musical education institution. 
 
 At the beginning of the 20th century, the Armenian professional performing culture was shaped. It was represented by the graduates of Russian and European conservatories. Violinist Hovhanness Nalbandyan, (St. Petersburg Conservatory and Berlin Musikhochschule), singer M.Akimova (Leipzig Conservatory), musicologist M.Aghayan (St. Petersburg and Moscow Conservatories), singer A.Shahmuradyan (Conservatoire de Paris) etc. 
 
 The founder and first rector of conservatory was famous Armenian composer and widely educated musician and public figure Romanos Melikyan (Saints-Petersburg Conservatory graduate)
